titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Black Oppressed People's Party Papers
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:26

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Royal Books on the Walter Jennings Memorial Fund, 2023; purchased from William Reese Company, on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book fund, 2024 March.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into three series: I. Business papers, 1970 - 1975. II: Printed material, 1968 - 1972. III. March 2024 Acquisition, 1968, undated.

bioghist.head: Black Oppressed People's Party
bioghist.p: Founded by Robert L. Gordon, later know as Harabu Tafadhali Himya, circa 1970, the Black Oppressed People's Party (BOPP) was an underground Black Power organization that allied with the Republic of New Afrika (RNA). RNA was a Black nationalist organization formed by members of the Malcolm X Society in Detroit in 1968; they espoused reparation settles and Black separatism with the explicit goal of creating an independent, Black-majority republic in the southeastern United States.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Black Oppressed People's Party papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains business papers, financial records, reports, memos, photographs, printed material, realia, and other papers created by or relating to the Black Oppressed People's Party (BOPP) and the Republic of New Afrika (RNA).

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Black Oppressed People's Party papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains business papers related to the Black Oppressed Peoples' Party including court records, financial records, and other materials.

did.unittitle: Magalis, Nougu releases and unlawful restraint
Himiya, Harabu Tatadhali: Speaking on genocide
Obadele, Imari Abubakari, I: Revolution and nation building strategy for building the black nation in America by Brother Imari
Republic of New Africa: Government adminstration
Republic of New Africa: Short official basic documents
Republic of New Africa: The struggle is for land featuring Imari Obadele President-Republic of New Africa
Republic of New Africa: Turn toward freedom
Republic of New Africa: UJAMAA Chicago Consulate Newsletter Vol. 1.2 No 1
Flier: We are asking you to vote for land and power

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a circular letter - signed by Milton R. Henry - announcing plans for the founding meeting of the Republic of New Africa.
